plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-607 RP19.P08.M06.S18-RP19.P09.M05.S17 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-607 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP19 p08 closeout p09 foundation: the P08 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, P08 Test And Golden Case Set, P08 Hermes Evidence Packet, P08 Claude Review Packet, P08 Closeout And Next Handoff, and the P09 foundation head through Permission And Audit Binding S17 as eleven descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows, with Hermes evidence (H19), Claude review packet (C19), and CP00-608 handoff, without opening client portal, secure link, client review, watermark runtime, state writes, or enterprise-trust claims from local validation.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-607 is a descriptor-only client-portal foundation pack (RP19.P08.M06.S18-RP19.P09.M05.S17, risk C, 150 units) and its claims are well-supported. The pack-level claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor — there is no over-claim. Verified: (1) scope/distribution consistency across prompt baseline, manifest, registry binding (CLIENT_PORTAL_CORE_CP607_*), live closeout-pack-plan pack, validator script, and tests — unit_count 150, range, deliverable_distribution {impl68,test9,hermes38,claude18,sec12,ui5}, and the 11 micro-title counts all match and sum to 150; (2) no-write/no-real-data — every no-write attestation flag is false, freezeCp607Result/freezeCp607Validation stamp synthetic_only/no_real_data/writes_product_state:false, validators enforce descriptor_only=true and runtime_execution=false on all rows, ROW_EXTRAS are boolean policy descriptors only, and a source grep found zero fs/network/child_process/db/process.env usage in packages/client-portal/src; (3) authority boundary — claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, and the hardened_review_sequence places adjudication/inspection/closeout AFTER the Claude run; (4) Hermes boundary — emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_packet is a descriptor with a receipt_shape string and H19 gate backed only by local npm validations; (5) review hardening — forbidden_review_evidence covers failed auth, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields, and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1450-byte stdout). All 7 required public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported via index.js; pre-review candidate validations in command-evidence all exit 0 (node --check x5, node --test, rp19/weighted/spec/rp00/rp05/goal/fullplan validates, npm test, npm run validate, build, git diff --check). Two P3 informational notes record expected post-review gating (deferred closeout-pack-plan:validate / closeout-pack:validate and not-yet-produced adjudication/inspection/review-result artifacts) that the human approver must confirm before commit. No P0/P1/P2 issues; Claude remains a read-only reviewer and human final approval governs any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: closeout-pack-plan:validate and closeout-pack:validate intentionally deferred to post-review
findings.details: Two of the 'validation commands expected before closeout' (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate and npm run closeout-pack:validate) are absent from the passed command-evidence list. command-evidence notes state this is intentional: closeout-pack-plan:validate is deferred until the post-review closeout-pack-plan:generate advances the live cursor from the CP00-606/CP00-607 boundary, and the pre-generation run reported the expected queue drift and is explicitly not counted as passed evidence. This is consistent with the live plan, where CP00-606 is the last live pack and CP00-607 is the first generated/queued pack. This is correct sequencing, not a defect, but it means full plan/pack closeout validation has not yet been demonstrated.
findings.recommendation: After this review passes, run closeout-pack-plan:generate (advancing the cursor) and confirm both closeout-pack-plan:validate and closeout-pack:validate exit 0 before committing the CP00-607 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/by-design per documented hardened_review_sequence; must complete in post-review closeout steps before commit. Non-blocking for the read-only review.

findings.title: Manifest evidence_refs reference post-review artifacts not yet present
findings.details: Every included_unit's evidence_refs point to docs/closeout-packs/cp00-607/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist yet (the cp00-607 docs directory currently contains only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is forward-looking evidence routing — unit status is 'in_progress', manifest production_ready is false, and command-evidence note 4 explicitly lists these as artifacts that 'must be produced and rerun before commit' — so it is not a false claim of existing evidence.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the canonical claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and review receipt are generated by the post-review pipeline (and the referenced paths resolve) before the pack is committed/closed.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-review candidate state; artifacts are generated by the post-review adjudication/inspection/receipt steps. Non-blocking for the read-only review.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-607 client portal core p08 closeout p09 foundation descriptor pack
